steps.title: Identify what the task requires
steps.content:
The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two dyeing approaches. A correct answer must contrast the traditional method (uses hazardous chemicals) with Rai's method (uses nanocellulose, a natural plant-based substance)—not describe only one approach or highlight a similarity.
  • "Though created using a different process, Rai's dye contains the same ingredient as the dye produced by blue jean manufacturers.": emphasizes a similarity (same ingredient, indigo), not a difference. ✗
  • "Nanocellulose is a natural, plant-based substance that separates the molecules of indigo powder, which doesn't dissolve in water.": explains what nanocellulose is without comparing the two approaches. ✗
  • "The traditional approach to dyeing blue jeans is to dip them in a solution containing hazardous chemicals.": describes only the traditional method without mentioning Rai's approach. ✗
  • "Rai's approach substitutes a natural, plant-based substance for the hazardous chemicals that manufacturers have traditionally used.": directly contrasts the two approaches by naming what Rai's method replaces (hazardous chemicals) and what it uses instead (natural, plant-based substance). ✓

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: “Rai's approach substitutes a natural, plant-based substance for the hazardous chemicals that manufacturers have traditionally used.” explicitly contrasts the two approaches in a single sentence, showing what changed between the traditional method and Rai's method. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
